KEN N ETH HAH N 
CHAIRMAN 
BOARD OF SUPERVISORS 
ILountll tlf lEos l\ngdl's 
1tj~nrh~ilf §tlpcr'tli~tlH& 
1fos l\ngl'll'&. ~,tlifornht poot'.! 
February 14, 1979 
Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Dear Mr. Lowenstein: 
866 HALL OF ADMINISTRATION 
974-2222 
MEMBERS OF THE BOARD 
PETER F SCHABARUM 
KENNETH HAHN 
EDMUND D. EDELMAN 
JAMES A HAYES 
BAXTER WARD 
I have been informed by our County Counsel that in filing 
the annual Statement of Economic Disclosure, certain values 
must be given to complimentary passes, whether or not they 
have been used. 
For example, an annual Disneyland pass has 
been valued by the Fair Political Practices Commission at 
$620. 
Before Disneyland was constructed, the late Walt Disney 
told me he was going to send me a pass. 
I attended the 
opening of Disneyland, and each year since, I have been sent 
a complimentary pass. 
Last year, I believe I used the pass 
once, and perhaps twice the year before. 
I have received a 
pass this year and I might not even go to the park. 
Wouldn't it be more fair and accurate to list the times I 
used the pass and value it accordingly? 
That would provide 
the real value as far as my use is concerned. 
The same 
practice would apply to reporting other complimentary passes 
which are sent to me unsolicited. 
It seems to me this prac-
tice would achieve truth-in-reporting in a simple and logical 
way_ 
KH:mw 
NNETH HAHN 
Chairman

sections.question: Wouldn't it be more fair and accurate to list the times I used the pass and value it accordingly? That would provide the real value as far as my use is concerned. The same practice would apply to reporting other complimentary passes which are sent to me unsolicited.
sections.question_synthetic: Should complimentary passes be valued based on actual usage rather than their full market value when filing annual Statements of Economic Disclosure?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice is provided in the document. The question is clear but appears in the body of the letter rather than in a formal section header.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Wouldn't it be more fair and accurate to list the times I used the pass and value it accordingly? That would provide the real value as far as my use is concerned. The same practice would apply to reporting other complimentary passes which are sent to me unsolicited.

EDELMAN JAMES A HAYES BAXTER WARD I have been informed by our County Counsel that in filing the annual Statement of Economic Disclosure, certain values must be given to complimentary passes, whether or not they have been used. For example, an annual Disneyland pass has been valued by the Fair Political Practices Commission at $620. Before Disneyland was constructed, the late Walt Disney told me he was going to send me a pass. I attended the opening of Disneyland, and each year since, I have been sent a complimentary pass. Last year, I believe I used the pass once, and perhaps twice the year before. I have received a pass this year and I might not even go to the park. embedding.summary: Kenneth Hahn, Chairman of the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors, inquires whether complimentary passes (such as an annual Disneyland pass valued at $620) should be reported based on actual usage rather than full market value on his Statement of Economic Disclosure.
